availableFacts: Lead notes mention 4.8 rating and 22 Google reviews
Lead notes include 276 5th Ave #704, New York, NY 10001, USA
missingData: No offer, warranty, emergency, certification, or licensing claims
designRead.artDirection: Neighbourhood field guide with a technical proof dossier layer: warm editorial paper, Manhattan contact context, precise HVAC-inspired linework, and phone-first conversion.
factualGuardrails: Do not invent emergency availability, same-day service, warranties, licensing, awards, years in business, service guarantees, or testimonials.
Treat service items as inquiry-routing categories unless later data verifies exact offerings.
Do not use unrelated stock photography or hotlinked assets.

typography.heroTarget: H1 should land in 2 to 3 desktop lines with a wide editorial measure and clamp-based sizing.
avoid: Arial or Helvetica as the identity
Raw system-ui as the main stack

customFonts.implementation: Define CSS variables in app/layout.tsx, then consume them in globals.css.
customFonts.fallback: Use serif/sans-serif fallbacks only after the custom font variables.

strategy: Use a text wordmark and CSS monogram/temperature dial.
Build a hero dossier panel with CSS linework, airflow curves, building-grid blocks, and contact tiles.
Use a later full-bleed CSS technical panel instead of fake photography.
If real business photos are later added to business.photos, use them as documentary accents and remove weaker CSS placeholders.

sections.name: Premium navigation
sections.purpose: Anchor navigation, brand identity, and persistent phone CTA.

sections.name: Hero dossier
sections.purpose: Art-directed first viewport with Manhattan HVAC positioning, phone CTA, website link, and factual data panel.

sections.name: Credibility strip
sections.purpose: Compact factual strip: Manhattan, HVAC, phone, website, rating/review note.

sections.name: Brand thesis
sections.purpose: Editorial two-column explanation of call-first HVAC contact readiness.

sections.name: Service architecture
sections.purpose: Interactive inquiry-routing categories for cooling, heating, airflow, and project coordination without pretending to be an exhaustive verified menu.

sections.name: Manhattan fit panel
sections.purpose: Asymmetric bento for building contexts, service area, address note, and contact path.

sections.name: Decision guide
sections.purpose: Practical checklist for what to prepare before and during the call.

sections.name: Process timeline
sections.purpose: Human process story from call to next step without promising timing or diagnosis.

sections.name: Expectation grid
sections.purpose: Proof and claim-boundary section using only supplied facts.

sections.name: Principles accordion
sections.purpose: Useful interaction covering factual claims, contact route, Manhattan context, and no-stock asset policy.

sections.name: Final contact CTA
sections.purpose: High-contrast conversion panel with phone and website.

sections.name: Footer
sections.purpose: Brand close, links, phone, website, service area, and factual-data note.

interactions.name: Anchor navigation
interactions.implementation: Use real section IDs and smooth scroll, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interactions.name: Service selector
interactions.implementation: Use accessible tabs if page becomes a client component, or native details panels for simpler buildability.

interactions.name: Principles accordion
interactions.implementation: Use native details/summary or labelled buttons with aria-controls.

interactions.name: Mobile sticky CTA
interactions.implementation: Fixed bottom bar below 760px with tel link and website link; add bottom padding to the page shell.

interactions.name: Hover and focus polish
interactions.implementation: Copper CTA states, cool-blue rule reveals, focus-visible rings, and reduced-motion-safe transitions.

ctaMap.omitted: No email CTA because no email exists in the business data.

breakpoints: Desktop >= 1024px: 12-column editorial grid and sticky header.
Tablet 760px-1023px: stacked hero with dossier panel preserved above the fold.
Mobile < 760px: compact header, one-column sections, horizontal anchor row if needed, sticky CTA.
layoutRules: Use overflow-x hidden on the page shell.
Use clamp for hero type and section spacing.
Convert bento and service architecture to one-column panels.
Reserve bottom padding so the sticky CTA never covers footer/contact content.
Wrap or relabel long website URLs to avoid overflow.

tasks: Create data helpers for phone, website, parsed address note, and rating/review note.
Define CSS variables, resets, focus states, and reduced-motion rules.
Rewrite page structure from blank canvas.
Build all planned sections in the chosen field-guide/dossier concept.
Avoid generic card-stack dominance and unsupported claims.
Implement service selector/details, principles accordion, anchor navigation, and mobile sticky CTA.
Add hover/focus polish without adding unnecessary dependencies.
Tune desktop, tablet, and mobile grids.
Check H1 line breaks, URL wrapping, CTA contrast, and sticky CTA spacing.
Run npm install if dependencies are unavailable.
Review screenshots at desktop and mobile sizes if browser tooling is available.
Rewrite weak scaffold-like sections before finalizing.

qaChecklist: At least 10 meaningful sections plus footer exist.
Hero is art-directed and not a centered generic SaaS block.
No unrelated stock images or hotlinked photos are used.
No fake emergency, warranty, license, award, years-in-business, or guarantee claims appear.
Rating/review count is shown only as lead-provided factual data, not as testimonials.
Services are framed as HVAC inquiry categories unless exact offerings are later supplied.
Every CTA points to a real phone or website path.
Mobile sticky CTA does not overlap contact/footer content.
Buttons and links have visible focus states and sufficient contrast.
The page could not be rebranded for another business by only changing the name.
